issues.description: Questions could benefit from more specific scaffolding for 6th grade students, such as sentence starters or word count guidance
Sample answers could include more specific examples of what constitutes appropriate evidence types for this grade level
Question 5 on evidence evaluation may be slightly advanced for early 6th grade - could benefit from more concrete language

strengths: Excellent alignment with RI.6.1 standard focusing on citing textual evidence and making inferences
Progressive difficulty from explicit evidence to inference and analysis
Clear structure with numbered tickets and consistent formatting
Appropriate academic vocabulary for grade level
Sample answers provide clear expectations for student responses
Questions require both citation skills and analytical thinking
Culturally neutral content accessible to diverse learners
overall_feedback: Strong exit ticket resource that effectively assesses student understanding of textual evidence and inference skills. The questions progress logically from basic citation to more complex analysis. Minor improvements in scaffolding and grade-level specificity would enhance usability.
